name: New Student Programs
summary: Baylor University's New Student Programs creates shared experiences that foster belonging and excitement for new students and that inspire them to take ownership of their unique Baylor story.

name: Pre-Law Society at Baylor
summary: Pre-Law Society at Baylor is an organization open to all students that intends to allow for better preparation necessary for those interested in a career in law.

name: Higher Education & Student Affairs Graduate Student Association
summary: HESA GSA strives to strengthen the culture of excellence within the HESA program; this is accomplished through engaging, developing, and building community amongst the past, present, and future students of the program.

name: Forensic Society at Baylor
summary: The Baylor Forensic Society is an organization that seeks to provide students with education, training, and hands-on experience with different scientific techniques used in forensic investigations, as well as fellowship and networking with others inter...

name: Phi Alpha Theta History Honor Society
summary: Organization created to encourage the study of history.

name: Baylor Chamber of Commerce
summary: A service organization dedicated to the promotion of, the service to, and the general welfare of Baylor University. This organization sponsors Homecoming, Diadeloso, the Baylor Line, the live Bear Mascot program, and Family Weekend.

name: Society of Automotive Engineers
summary: The purpose of the organization is to allow undergraduate students to explore the world of engineering while gaining hands-on experience in design, manufacturing, and testing. All students are welcome and no prior experience is necessary.

name: Hankamer Student Organization
summary: HSO serves as the direct link between the Dean’s office and Business Students. Students support the Business School throughout the year and some volunteer opportunities include Fall/Spring Premiere, Welcome Week Mixer, Distinguished Scholar Days.

name: Student Chapter for the Society of Environmental Toxicology and Chemistry (SETAC) at Baylor University
summary: To promote research, education, and development in the areas of environmental science, environmental toxicology, environmental chemistry, remediation, and risk management.

name: Graduate Student Association
summary: The Baylor University Graduate Student Association is a community that strives to enrich the quality of academic and social life for students of each graduate program of Baylor University as well as advocate the rights of these students.

name: Geological Society at Baylor University
summary: Promote student involvement in both academic and industrial geology opportunities, as well as connect members with professionals in their fields for learning, collaboration, and employment opportunities.

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: The Society of Physics Students encourages the interest in physics throughout the academic and local communities and introduces student members into the professional community. Our chapter is affiliated with the American Institute of Physics.

name: Kappa Omicron Nu
summary: National honor society for family and consumer sciences majors. Promote scholarship and encourage intellectual development, promote research and foster the spirit of inquiry, confer distinction for high achievement, promote leadership development, stim...

name: Möbius Mathematics Society
summary: Our mission at Möbius is to create a fun and engaging environment for math enthusiasts, foster connections to new mathematical ideas and applications, and enhance career awareness within and beyond academia.

name: Neuroscience Student Association
summary: The Neuroscience Student Association (NSA) is the perfect organization for students who are fascinated by the inner workings of the brain and want to be able to discuss them in a relaxed setting with like-minded people.

name: Interior Design Association at Baylor
summary: An organization that allows students to network with professionals in the interior design field and be a part of the professional organizations of ASID, and IIDA,. This organization provides resources and insight to students for post-graduation.

name: Physician Assistant Society
summary: The purpose of the organization is to promote the Physician Assistant profession as a viable choice for all students interested in a health care career and to provide each member with a thorough understanding of the role of a PA.

name: Pi Tau Sigma
summary:
Mechanical Engineering Honor Society at Baylor University.

To establish a closer bond of fellowship among our members which will result in mutual benefit to those men and women in the study and profession of Mechanical Engineering.

name: Beta Beta Beta
summary: Beta Beta Beta (TriBeta) is a National Biology Honor Society for students, particularly undergraduates, dedicated to improving the understanding and appreciation of biological study and extending boundaries of human knowledge through scientific research.

name: Apparel Professionals
summary: To promote professionalism among students studying in the field of apparel and to provide educational opportunities that go beyond the classroom environment.

name: Association for Computing Machinery
summary: ACM aims to promote an increased knowledge of the science, design, development, construction languages, and applications of modern computing machinery.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta
summary: Alpha Epsilon Delta is Baylor’s only Health Preprofessional Honor Society. Our organization seeks to foster rich community, servant leadership, and academic excellence through our events and programs.

name: Psi Chi
summary: Psi Chi is an international honor society whose purpose shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology.

name: Graduate Association of School Psychology
summary: To facilitate professional development and fellowship for graduate students and alumni of the School Psychology Program at Baylor.

name: Psychology Club
summary: Psychology Club is an academic club that serves Psychology majors by aiding in retention, mutual support in completing the major, and the formulation of specific career, job, and internship goals.

name: Pre-Optometry Professional Society at Baylor University
summary: Pre-Optometry Professional Society at Baylor University is an undergraduate pre-health organization founded to serve those interested in the field of optometry and provide the resources needed to facilitate the application process for optometry school.

name: Graduate Business Association
summary: The Graduate Business Association (GBA) is a student-run organization that aims to improve the experience of current and future graduate business students by providing educational, networking, and personal growth opportunities.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
summary: Professional organization that provides communication with professional public relations practitioners and informs its members of current trends and job opportunities in the PR field.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
summary: A co-ed professional business fraternity. Activities include professional speakers, service projects for Baylor and the community, alumni events, and social functions.

name: IEEE - Microwave Theory and Technology Society
summary: The purpose of the Microwave Theory and Technology Society (MTT-Society) is to introduce students to the work that is being done in the fields related to microwave technology. This includes work being done on WiFi, radar, 5G, and radios, among others.

name: Kappa Kappa Psi
summary: We in Kappa Kappa Psi are brothers that serve and promote the university band programs at Baylor. Our missions are to cultivate the growth of our members through genuine friendships that last as long as stars shall shine and broaden the musical community

name: Aim Higher
summary: Aim Higher promotes professional development and community service allowing members to grow as future Air Force officers, give back to our community, and fellowship.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary: The mission of the National Society of Black Engineers is "to increase the number of culturally responsible Black Engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community."​

name: National Association of Black Journalists
summary: To provide quality programs and services to and advocate on behalf of black journalists.The organization has worked for diversity and to increase the number of minorities in newsrooms across the country.

name: National Society of Collegiate Scholars
summary: The Baylor Chapter of NSCS is a unique home for students of all majors. We focus on scholarship, leadership, and service by providing outreach opportunities for those involved. Look for an invitation from the National Organization before joining!

name: Silver Wings
summary: Silver Wings is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education about national defense.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
summary: An international business fraternity organized to foster the study of business in universities. Co-educational and open to all business majors. Activities include professional programs, service projects, and social activities.

name: Nu Rho Psi
summary: To encourage and recognize excellence in scholarship, especially in neuroscience, to promote leadership and encourage intellectual and social interaction between students, faculty and professionals.

name: National Student Speech-Language-Hearing Association
summary: Organization for students interested in communication sciences and the speech pathology, audiology profession. Membership includes social, service, and advocacy events.

name: Sociology of Undergraduate Lives (SOUL)
summary: Our purpose is to provide a new method of applying Sociological concepts outside of the classroom. We aim to serve Sociology students looking for a community they can deeply invest in while diving deeper into their area of study.

name: Roots Natural Hair Club
summary: The purpose of Roots shall be to foster community among men and women with natural hair, educate all students about hair science as it pertains to natural hair, and to celebrate the role of hair in the African American culture.

name: Eta Kappa Nu
summary: Eta Kappa Nu (HKN) is the honor society within IEEE for high-achieving juniors, seniors, and all graduate students in ECE. The goal of the Kappa Tau chapter of HKN is to promote leadership, scholarship, and professional development.

name: Baylor Ambassadors
summary: Advocate on issues impacting students at Baylor on the local, state, and federal level as well as act as a liaison between Baylor and political dignitaries visiting campus

name: North Village Heritage House - Fine Arts LLC
summary: Home of the Fine Arts LLC, Heritage House is an apartment style residence hall open to freshmen - senior students.

name: The Pulse Honors Undergraduate Journal
summary: The Pulse is the undergraduate journal of the Honors College, featuring multidisciplinary student research in annually published issues. The Pulse recognizes and develops exemplary research and writing through a rigorous evaluation and editing cycle.

name: Men's Club Ultimate Frisbee
summary: We're Stonewall, the men's club ultimate frisbee team at Baylor University! We represent Baylor in intercollegiate competitions with schools like Texas A&M, UT, and Texas Tech.

name: University Parks Apartments
summary: University Parks is home to over 500 on-campus residents of all majors. Residents enjoy building strong relationships through social and spiritual programming that make this large community feel more close-knit!

name: American Choral Directors Association, Student Chapter at Baylor University
summary: To promote the profession of Choral Music Education at Baylor University through seminars, conversations, and connections.

name: Allen and Dawson Residence Hall - LEAD LLC
summary: The Leadership Living Learning Center (LEAD LLC) is a four-year residential experience that challenges and equips students to live a more meaningful life, and to influence others to make a positive impact on their world. LEAD cultivates students' leade...

name: Association for Women in Mathematics
summary: AWM's purpose is to encourage women to study, and have active careers in, the mathematical sciences, and to promote equal treatment and opportunities within the field. Our mission is to facilitate activities that support this purpose, both at the gradu...

name: The Alexander Hamilton Society
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society (AHS) is a non-partisan, non-profit national organization that seeks to identify, educate, and launch young people into foreign policy and national security careers imbued with a perspective of principled U.S. leadership.

name: Bears' Association of Human Resources
summary: The Bears' Association of Human Resources (BAHR) is a student chapter of the Society for Human Resource Management, sponsored by Dallas HR. BAHR meets twice monthly, offering events that enhance HR knowledge, foster networking, and successful careers!

name: Brooks Flats Residence Hall - Business and Innovation LLC
summary: Residence Hall at Baylor University

name: Brooks Residential College
summary: Brooks Residential College is modelled on years of successful and traditional living learning programs such as those found at Oxford, Cambridge, Harvard and Yale. As the first and oldest residential college on Baylor’s campus, Brooks is an established,...

name: Union Board
summary: The Union Board exists to create and sustain programs, within the Bill Daniel Student Center, that are both engaging and enriching in order to foster community and relationships among one another.

name: Latinx Association of Science and Health
summary: The purpose of this organization is to empower Latinx students to go make an impact in their community based on the resources and experiences that we provide to them through membership.

name: CURE BU
summary: CURE International is a non-profit organization that operates charitable hospitals and programs in 29 countries worldwide where patients experience the life-changing message of God’s love for them, receiving surgical treatment regardless of gender, rel...

name: ACM-W Student Chapter at Baylor University
summary: Our mission is to establish a community dedicated to empowering and cultivating the growth of women in the field of technology.

name: Investment and Advisory Network at Baylor University
summary: I&A

name: Association for Women Geoscientists
summary: Baylor Association for Women Geoscientists (BAWG) was established in the spring of 2018. Its goal is to provide a supportive and encouraging environment for all members to promote the professional development of women in geoscience related fields.

name: Videogame Club at Baylor University
summary: The Videogame Club at Baylor University, founded in 2017, is a student organization focused on connecting and bringing together Baylor students by hosting events centered on playing video games.

name: Network of Enlightened Women
summary: NeW is a national, non-profit organization for conservative university women! NeW members meet to debate and discuss politics, gender, the media, and conservatism whilst cultivating community and expanding the intellectual diversity on campus.

name: Artistic Events
summary: The Creative Arts Experience (CAE) engages students in the fine arts at Baylor providing enriching programs of art, music, theatre, film, and literature.

name: Her Campus Baylor
summary: Her Campus Baylor is a chapter of Her Campus, the #1 media site for college women worldwide. We strive to empower women through our words, actions and attitudes while engaging the Baylor and Waco community through media content and events.

name: Online MBA Program
summary: The Baylor Online MBA program is designed for working professionals who desire high education standards with a flexible schedule.

name: Association for Supply Chain Management
summary: Baylor ASCM exists to enhance students' experience in the supply chain profession by exposing students to professional development opportunities which provide expertise on the ever-developing industry.

name: Graphic Design Student Association
summary: A professional development organization for students pursuing careers in graphic design

name: Theatre Student Society
summary: The Baylor University Theatre Student Society aims to support and benefit the Department of Theatre Arts in various ways, while also providing exciting events for theatre students.

name: Healthcare is Ours
summary:
Healthcare is Ours is a mentorship program that aims to increase diversity in healthcare by
encouraging and equipping minorities in Waco ISD.

name: Medlife
summary: MEDLIFE is a  non-profit organization that partners with low-income communities in Latin America and Africa to improve their access to medicine, education, and community development projects.

name: Ice Hockey Club
summary: The official Baylor Ice Hockey team page. Tryouts for the 25-26 season will be held on Friday, September 4. Please fill out the interest form located on our website.

name: Gamma Iota Sigma
summary: .Academic Organization focused on providing resources and professional development for students pursuing a career in the industry of Risk Management and Insurance

name: Caribbean Student Association
summary: To foster a community where Caribbean students are able to share their unique culture, support one another academically, socially and culturally, and serve as an information and support center for all students with primary focus on Caribbean origins.

name: Poetry Club
summary: Our mission is to create a safe space to explore the human experience through the creative medium of poetry. This is a place to learn about poetry, express yourself using it, and gain new ways and techniques to do so.

name: Startup and Innovation Club
summary: Providing student creatives with the resources to build a strong foundation for their entrepreneurial endeavors.

name: Data Science Club at Baylor University
summary: The purpose of this club will be to connect students who have an interest in Data Science or whose end goal is a career in Data Science, regardless of their major or chosen discipline.

name: Lambda Pi Eta
summary: Lambda Pi Eta (LPH) is the National Communication Association’s official honor society for four-year colleges and universities. The purpose of LPH is to promote outstanding academic achievement in the Communication discipline.

name: Warfighter Weightlifting Club
summary: The purpose of the Warfighter Weightlifting Club is to provide a unique avenue for fitness here at Baylor, to provide members an insight into ROTC programs here on campus, and to promote a culture of fitness and personal growth for all those involved.

name: Pi Epsilon
summary: The purpose of the Society is to promote the study of environmental sciences through recognition of exemplary scholarly and professional activity.

name: Bears With T1D
summary: This organization is designed to be a support system for individuals battling Type 1 Diabetes on Baylor's campus. We come together in order to create lifelong bonds through social events and community.

name: SACNAS at Baylor
summary: SACNAS at Baylor an inclusive source of community with the mission of supporting underrepresented minorities in attaining advanced degrees, careers, and positions of leadership in STEM.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Inc.
summary: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of a historically black college. On the evening of November 17, 1911, Omega Psi Phi was founded at Howard University.

name: Global Health Student Network
summary:
The purpose of GHSN is to promote global healthcare equity on the domestic and international stage by uniting students through global missions and global health dialogue. 

Follow our Instagram: @baylorglobalhealth
Contact us: baylorghsn@gmail.com

name: Music Industry Club
summary: Our mission is to promote and market student musicians at Baylor. This club combines business and marketing to act as a record label on campus. We strive to give student artists a head start within production, networking & marketing prior to graduation.

name: The Women's Network
summary: To cultivate and celebrate women's ambition through connecting our members to industry leaders, professional development resources, and career opportunities.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
summary: SASE promotes diversity and inclusion of Asian heritage students in the STEM field by offering professional development, networking, and service opportunities!

name: Chi Sigma Alpha Student Affairs Honor Society
summary: Our goal is to contribute to the academic excellence, continuing research, and professional status of the student affairs profession.

name: Universities Allied for Essential Medicines
summary: Universities Allied For Essential Medicines at Baylor is committed to improving access to medications for individuals who cannot afford them or those who face barriers to access.

name: Bear AI
summary: The purpose of Bear AI is to educate and foster discussion about artificial intelligence among Baylor students.

name: Biology CMHD and EEO Graduate Student Society
summary: To promote camaraderie among graduate students within the biology department at Baylor and to provide members with an outlet to practice presentations, participate in community outreach, and increase communication between faculty and graduate students.

name: Cybersecurity Club at Baylor
summary: This organization exists to promote cybersecurity awareness and education on campus.

name: Student Organ Donor Advocates
summary: SODA, or the Student Organ Donor Advocates, is an organization dedicated to educating others on the impact of becoming a deceased organ donor and the ease of registering as a donor.

name: Real Estate Network at Baylor
summary: Our club's mission statement: To Educate, Inspire, and Advise Baylor students pursuing a career in the Real Estate Industry.

name: Upsilon Pi Epsilon Honor Society
summary: UPE is the only international honor society exclusively for computing and information disciplines and is proudly affiliated with prestigious organizations such as the ACM and IEEE. Only the top students from each class are invited to become members.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) at Baylor University is dedicated to fostering a supportive and inclusive community for Hispanic and Latino engineers.

name: Astro at Baylor
summary: This organization allows students to gain hands-on rocket engineering experience through project and competition teams.

name: Pre-Law Chapter of National Black Law Students Association
summary: The Baylor Pre-Law Chapter of National Black Law Students Association is committed to creating a space for black students at Baylor University that are interested in law and legal studies, promoting professionalism, community service, and leadership.

name: Makers at BU
summary: Makers at BU facilitates an environment for Baylor students to gather, discuss and collaborate on various personal or group projects in the fields of technology, engineering and digital or electronic art.

name: Gift of Life/Baylor Chapter
summary: Gift of Life's purpose is to cure blood cancer and related diseases through cellular therapies by connecting patients in need with life-saving donors.

name: Financial Literacy Mentors at Baylor
summary: The purpose of Financial Literacy Mentors at Baylor is to educate and mentor middle and high school students on essential financial principles by connecting them with Baylor University students.

name: Nigerian Student Association
summary: The Nigerian Students Association of Baylor University exists to support and advance the social, cultural, academic, and professional needs and interests of prospective, incoming, current, and former Nigerian students of Baylor University.

name: Professional Writing and Rhetoric Student Organization
summary: The PWR Student Organization aims to build community among students majoring in Professional Writing and Rhetoric, and among other students who are interested in PWR.

name: Scientists Promoting Awareness, Research, and Knowledge
summary: The purpose of SPARK is to empower graduate students and postdoctoral scholars in science at Baylor University

name: Waco Symphony Orchestra Student Ambassadors
summary: WSO Student Ambassadors unite Baylor students with the Waco Symphony to advance local arts philanthropy, drive campus engagement, and harness personal and professional growth through nonprofit support and music advocacy.

name: Bridgebuilding Fellows Program
summary: N/A
